criteria.notes: Extensively uses @amsterdam/design-system-react: Page, PageHeader, PageFooter, Grid, Column, Heading, Paragraph, Button, Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint.
Custom CSS uses --ams-space-l, --ams-space-s, --ams-typography-body-text-bold-font-weight tokens; token CSS imported in main.tsx.
Uses Grid and Grid.Cell with span narrow:4/medium:8/wide:9 and narrow:4/medium:8/wide:3 for main+sidebar layout visible in screenshot.
PageHeader with Gemeente Amsterdam logo and brandName visible; PageFooter with MenuLinks at bottom of screenshot.
Newsletter form uses ADS Field, Label, TextInput, ErrorMessage, Hint, and Button components.
No banned libraries used; only Amsterdam Design System and React Router as dependencies.
All visible text in screenshot is Dutch: headings, paragraphs, navigation, form labels, footer links.
Generally clear but contains complex compound words like 'kennisinstellingen', 'bouwwerkzaamheden', 'maatschappelijke functies' that exceed B1 level.
Each of the 3 pages has exactly 5 Paragraph components; 5 paragraphs visible on overview page in screenshot.
References real Zuidas projects: Zuidasdok, A10 Zuid, station Amsterdam Zuid, Gustav Mahlerlaan, Ravel development.
Consistent formal municipal tone using 'u' form across all pages, professional and informative.
Three routes defined via React Router: / (OverviewPage), /projecten (ProjectsPage), /leefbaarheid (LivingPage).
Newsletter email form clearly visible in screenshot with label, input, and submit button; included on all 3 pages.
Sidebar navigation with 3 NavLink items visible in screenshot with active-state bold styling.
